action.hypothesis: Matched architecture probe on `lowrank_jumprelu_residual` under the unchanged mainline recipe (`K=128, EF=2, signum, lr=3.0e-3`). Final reconstruction is `x_hat = U(Vx) + Σ_i alpha_i b_i`, where `U(Vx)` is a static low-rank trunk and the JumpReLU selector only changes which residual dictionary atoms `b_i` are activated and their coefficients. This is `扩展兼容`: LUTurbo/Lottable can export the trunk factors and residual dictionary as static vector libraries, then execute `trunk lookup + thresholded sparse selection + weighted lookup-sum` online. The probe asks one question only: in the low-EF feasible regime, does learned thresholding provide a better architecture tradeoff than the current soft-codebook two-stage mainline, or should this family be ruled out. Encoder selection cost should stay in the feasible band at `EF=2`, so this should produce a real signal instead of a policy reject.
action.summary: Probe `lowrank_jumprelu_residual` as the one matched architecture change. It is LUTurbo-exportable (`扩展兼容`) through a static low-rank trunk plus static residual dictionary, and it should give a real low-cost signal on whether learned thresholding is worth continuing versus the current soft-codebook two-stage mainline.
action.self_review: Single-variable discipline is preserved: only `ARCHITECTURE` changes while `K`, `EXPANSION_FACTOR`, `OPTIMIZER`, and `LR` remain fixed to the mainline recipe. This probe avoids another likely budget rejection and tests a remaining compatible architecture that can still deliver a clean go/no-go answer for the low-EF frontier.
notes_to_memory: `lowrank_jumprelu_residual` is LUTurbo `扩展兼容`: decode remains a finite weighted sum over a static low-rank trunk plus a static residual dictionary; JumpReLU only changes selector-side activation sparsity.
This matched probe stays inside the likely feasible encoder-cost regime and is more informative than another routed/bucketed-style over-budget probe.
When scoring the run, inspect `tokens_per_sec`, wall-clock time, and `final_total_tokens` together with FVU; a tiny quality gain with slower training is a negative signal.
next_hypotheses: Return to the mainline `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` next round and tune exactly one cost knob such as `TRUNK_RANK`, `NUM_CODES`, or `STAGE1_RATIO`.

If `lowrank_jumprelu_residual` underperforms the mainline on FVU or shows materially worse `tokens_per_sec`/runtime without compensating benefit, mark the family as not worth continuing in the low-cost regime.
